title: Pacing & Performance
description: Get display advertising pacing metrics including impressions, budget, CTR, ROAS, and delivery pace data - matching the Dashboard's Display Summary page.

steps.title: Understand pacing data
steps.description: Pacing data provides real-time campaign performance metrics including impressions delivered, budget spent, CTR, ROAS, and delivery pace projections. This data powers the Dashboard's Display Summary > Pacing tab.

steps.title: Query pacing metrics with filters
steps.description: Fetch pacing data for campaigns with filters matching Dashboard usage. The Dashboard typically filters by status (LIVE campaigns) and orders by most recent.
steps.query:
query GetCampaignPacingMetrics($first: Int, $where: CampaignOrderWhereInput, $orderBy: CampaignOrderOrderByInput) {
  campaignOrdersConnection(first: $first, where: $where, orderBy: $orderBy) {
    edges {
      node {
        id
        name
        status
        startDate
        endDate
        budget
        pacingData {
          id
          campaignName
          totalImpressions
          impressionGoal
          impressionsToday
          impressionsYesterday
          impressionsLeft
          expectedImpressions
          dailyAvgImpressions
          dailyAvgImpressionsLeft
          ctr
          roas
          budgetSpent
          totalBudget
          budgetLeft
          dailyAvgBudget
          dailyAvgBudgetLeft
          daysLeft
          daysIntoFlight
          pace
          paceYesterday
          paceTwoDaysAgo
          paceThreeDaysAgo
          paceFourDaysAgo
          paceFiveDaysAgo
          paceSixDaysAgo
          paceSevenDaysAgo
        }
      }
    }
    aggregate {
      count
    }
    pageInfo {
      hasNextPage
      endCursor
    }
  }
}
steps.tip: Filter by status_in: LIVE for active campaigns, PAST for finished campaigns, APPROVED for pending launch, or combine multiple values.

steps.title: Filter by date range and advertiser
steps.description: The Dashboard defaults to a 6-month date range. You can also filter by advertiser for multi-tenant organizations.
steps.tip: Use startDate_gte and endDate_lte for date range filtering. The Dashboard defaults to 6 months of data.

steps.title: Understand pacing fields
steps.description:
Key metrics in the pacing data:

**Delivery Metrics:**
- **totalImpressions**: Impressions delivered so far
- **impressionGoal**: Target impressions for the campaign
- **impressionsLeft**: Remaining impressions to deliver
- **dailyAvgImpressions**: Average daily impressions needed

**Budget Metrics:**
- **budgetSpent / totalBudget / budgetLeft**: Budget tracking
- **dailyAvgBudget / dailyAvgBudgetLeft**: Daily budget pacing

**Performance:**
- **ctr**: Click-through rate (percentage)
- **roas**: Return on ad spend

**Pacing:**
- **pace**: Today's delivery pace (100 = on track)
- **paceYesterday** through **paceSevenDaysAgo**: Historical pace
- **daysLeft / daysIntoFlight**: Campaign timeline
steps.tip: Pace values: 100% = on track, >100% = ahead of schedule, <100% = behind schedule.

steps.title: Filter by campaign ID
steps.description: Query pacing data for a specific campaign by filtering the connection.
steps.tip: Using campaignOrdersConnection with an ID filter works with all permission levels.

tips: Common status values: LIVE, LIVE_PENDING, LIVE_APPROVED, PAUSED, PAUSED_PENDING, PAUSED_APPROVED, APPROVED, PAST, DRAFT, LAUNCHING_LIVE, LAUNCHING_PAUSED
pace value of 100 means on track, >100 means ahead of schedule, <100 means behind
ctr (Click-Through Rate) indicates ad engagement quality
roas (Return on Ad Spend) shows revenue generated per dollar spent
dailyAvgImpressionsLeft helps predict if campaigns will hit their goals
Historical pace fields (paceYesterday, paceTwoDaysAgo, etc.) show delivery trends
Use orderBy: startDate_DESC to show most recent campaigns first
Use aggregate.count to get total matching campaigns
